title: Vast names Isakowitz as a senior adviser
summary: Vast appointed the former president and CEO of The Aerospace Corp. as a senior adviser. The move comes as Vast awaits the next phase of a NASA program for its commercial space station.

title: SSC Space, Firefly set 2028 target for first orbital launch from Sweden’s Esrange
summary: SSC Space and Firefly aim to conduct the first orbital launch from Sweden's Esrange Space Center by 2028, as critical infrastructure and regulatory frameworks are being established.

title: Orbital files plans for 100,000 orbital data centers
summary: Startup Orbital has filed an FCC application to deploy up to 100,000 data center satellites, aiming to provide 10 gigawatts of computing power from space to meet AI demand. This ambitious project seeks to revolutionize in-space data processing.

title: Study argues bigger launch vehicles may not always be better
summary: A new study suggests that extremely large launch vehicles may not always be the optimal choice for space missions, despite industry trends favoring them. The research challenges the assumption that bigger rockets are inherently better.

title: ESA to seek lunar mapping capability for Argonaut lander
summary: ESA will use external lunar topographic data for the design phase of its Argonaut lunar lander, potentially including its first mission. The agency is seeking lunar mapping capabilities to support this effort.

title: Beck: Iridium acquisition the “logical next step” for Rocket Lab
summary: Rocket Lab CEO Peter Beck described the acquisition of Iridium as the 'logical next step' to expand into the space services market. The move aligns with Rocket Lab's strategic growth ambitions.

title: Discovering the Universe – Astrophysics Flagship Space Observatories Offer Insights to Key Questions
summary: BAE Systems is enabling cutting-edge technology for NASA's astrophysics flagship space observatories, which aim to observe distant galaxies and explore exoplanets and their atmospheres. This partnership expands our knowledge of the universe.

title: Quantum computing is about to become a national security problem in orbit
summary: Quantum computing's rapid advancement threatens modern encryption, posing a national security risk that directly impacts the commercial space industry.

title: Rocket Lab to acquire Iridium
summary: Rocket Lab is acquiring satellite telecommunications company Iridium for $8 billion to expand its end-to-end space capabilities. This acquisition marks a significant step in Rocket Lab's growth strategy within the space industry.

title: SatVu restarts commercial operations with HotSat-2
summary: SatVu has resumed commercial operations with its HotSat-2 satellite, following the failure of its debut satellite in low Earth orbit in 2023. The British thermal imaging startup now offers revenue-generating services.

title: Europe's deadly heat wave seen from space | Space photo of the day for June 30, 2026
summary: Europe's deadly heat wave was captured from space by the Sentinel-3 satellite, providing data to analyze the extent of soaring temperatures. The imagery aids researchers in understanding the impact of extreme weather events.

title: 'Titan is actually a very reasonable destination for humans': Scientists start mapping out crewed mission to huge Saturn moon
summary: Scientists are planning a crewed mission to Titan, Saturn's largest moon, with the first 'Humans to Titan Summit 2026' held to discuss feasibility. Titan is considered a viable destination for human exploration due to its unique environment.

title: NASA will announce moon base news today: Watch it live
summary: NASA will announce updates about its moon base plans in a live announcement this afternoon. The event can be watched in real-time.

title: 'The start of a new era': Rocket Lab buying satellite-communications company Iridium for $8 billion
summary: Rocket Lab is acquiring satellite-communications company Iridium for $8 billion, marking a significant expansion in the space industry. The deal represents one of the largest transactions in the sector, highlighting growing consolidation trends.

title: It's been 25 years since 'A.I. Artificial Intelligence', and we think this was peak Spielberg sci-fi
summary: It marks the 25th anniversary of the film 'A.I. Artificial Intelligence', directed by Steven Spielberg, which is considered a peak in sci-fi cinema. The movie is a collaboration between two cinematic geniuses.

title: Watch 2 NASA astronauts fix ISS' huge robotic arm during spacewalk today
summary: NASA astronauts Chris Williams and Jessica Meir will perform a spacewalk today to repair the International Space Station's Canadarm2. The event can be watched live.

title: 'I'm still a Trekkie at heart': 'Starseeker: Astroneer Expeditions' takes space exploration games in a friendlier direction (interview)
summary: The follow-up game 'Starseeker: Astroneer Expeditions' is taking space exploration games in a friendlier direction, diverging from traditional expectations. It aims to appeal to a broader audience, including casual gamers and fans of the 'Star Trek' franchise.

title: NASA to launch rescue mission July 1 to save Swift space telescope from burning up in Earth's atmosphere
summary: NASA plans to launch a rescue mission on July 1 to prevent the Swift space telescope from burning up in Earth's atmosphere. Northrop Grumman will deploy a rocket from an airplane to save the telescope.

title: This star's light has been crossing space since the American Revolution. Here's how to find it
summary: A star's light, which left its surface around the time of the American Revolution, can still be observed today. Scientists explain how to locate it.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope, marking its first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, providing data to aid future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The image highlights the telescope's capabilities in studying dense stellar regions.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 also saw new work as part of ongoing development.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. This marks a significant milestone in its transition to an educational exhibit after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launch schedule features six orbital launches, including the debut of Pegasus XL. It will occur alongside missions by Falcon 9 and Chinese launchers.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test marks a significant step in developing advanced reentry technology.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This follows a busy month of activities aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing its Terran R heavy-lift rocket program, with the second stage arriving at Stennis Space Center as the company targets a 2026 debut. This marks a key step in the development of the fully reusable launch vehicle.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches are expected to take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of space exploration and innovation.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its potential to revolutionize satellite servicing and maintenance. The technology is expected to enhance operational efficiency in orbit.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a return to routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping ESA test and select materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these researchers to support the agency's missions.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. This collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satellite communications market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This move marks a significant expansion for Rocket Lab beyond launch services.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from Sweden's Esrange Space Center in 2028 under a new franchise model. The collaboration with SSC Space marks Firefly's expansion into international markets.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: ISS Risk Margin ‘Alarming’ As NASA Nears 2030 Extension Date
summary: The ISS faces increasing operational risks as NASA approaches the 2030 extension date, with the risk margin for regular operations described as 'alarming' by a NASA advisory committee chair.

title: SpaceX Alum Have Raised $9.2B, New Data Shows
summary: SpaceX alumni have collectively raised $9.2 billion in funding, highlighting its role as a talent incubator for high-level engineers and innovators. The company's 24-year history has spawned numerous new ventures and ideas.

title: Firefly Acquires Autonomous Space Ops Company
summary: Firefly Aerospace acquired Space-ng, an AI-powered vision navigation and autonomous guidance systems provider. This acquisition aims to enhance Firefly's autonomous space operations capabilities.

title: Europe Expands its Launch Opportunities to New Companies
summary: Europe is increasing launch opportunities for new companies to enhance its space industry competitiveness. This move aims to meet growing demand and foster innovation in the sector.

title: Exclusive: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic Partner on Security Demo Mission
summary: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic have partnered to conduct a security demonstration mission. The collaboration aims to address hacking threats and shared-compute modules in rideshare satellites.

title: New Megaconstellation Startup Eclipse Space Targets Sovereign Market
summary: Eclipse Space, a new megaconstellation startup founded by SpaceX alumni, has emerged from stealth mode to target sovereign governments and enterprises for constellation ownership. The company aims to make space-based connectivity accessible to sovereign markets.

title: Saudia and Neo Space Group Linefit Inaugural Airbus A321XLR Aircraft With IFC
summary: Saudia and Neo Space Group have inaugurated the first line-fit activation of NSG Skywaves In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) on Saudia's Airbus A321XLR aircraft. The system is powered by NSG Skywaves and utilizes SES's satellite network.

title: SiriusXM Continues Refresh With SXM-11 Launch
summary: SpaceX launched the SXM-11 satellite for SiriusXM, which will be the highest power satellite in SiriusXM's fleet. This mission is part of SiriusXM's ongoing satellite refresh efforts.

title: Rocket Lab’s End-to-End Vision Comes Into Focus With ‘Transformative’ Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab announced a transformative deal to acquire Iridium Communications, advancing its goal of becoming an end-to-end space company. The acquisition integrates launch, manufacturing, and satellite services under one organization.

title: Omar Qaise Looks to Build OQ Technology Into a Sovereign European Pioneer of D2D
summary: OQ Technology, led by CEO and founder Omar Qaise, aims to establish itself as a sovereign European leader in direct-to-device (D2D) technology as the market grows. The company seeks to become a trusted partner for European governments in this emerging sector.

title: Commercial Markets Are Ready for EO — But the Understanding Gap Persists
summary: The EO Summit in London highlighted that commercial markets are prepared for Earth Observation (EO) solutions, but there is a persistent challenge in effectively communicating their benefits to customers.

title: Calian to Acquire Canadian Service Provider Galaxy Broadband
summary: Calian Group is set to acquire Galaxy Broadband Communications for up to $52 million CAD ($36.6 million USD) to expand its satellite communications and remote connectivity solutions. The deal marks a strategic move in the Canadian space industry.

title: Tototheo Global to Deploy SES FlexMaritime in Expanded Maritime Deal
summary: Tototheo Global has expanded its agreement with SES to deploy the FlexMaritime connectivity service for the maritime market. The service combines SES's satellite network with integrated solutions.

title: ReOrbit Secures Finnish Funding for ‘OrbitCloud’ R&D Program
summary: ReOrbit has secured 4.6 million euro ($5.3 million) in Finnish funding for its 'OrbitCloud' R&D program, which aims to develop a space-based edge computing network. The initiative is supported by Business Finland.

title: AiRanaculus to Advance Networking Technologies for NASA Lunar and Space Communications Networks
summary: AiRanaculus received a $5 million award from NASA to develop networking technologies for lunar and space communications networks. The technology will support NASA's future lunar and deep space missions.
